key_questions: Atopic dermatitis (personal/family history of atopy, chronic/relapsing, typical distribution)?
Allergic contact dermatitis (ACD): exposure to latex, nickel, fragrances, preservatives, hair dye?
Irritant contact dermatitis (ICD): repeated exposure to water, detergents, solvents (most common — healthcare workers)?
Distribution matches exposure (hands for ICD/ACD; generalized for atopic)?
red_flags: Secondary bacterial infection (weeping/yellow crusting, fever) → S. aureus
Eczema herpeticum (widespread punched-out vesicles in eczema) → HSV emergency
Generalized erythroderma (>90% BSA) → systemic treatment needed
Moderate-severe uncontrolled eczema → consider biologic (dupilumab) — specialist referral
ddx: Seborrheic dermatitis (scalp, nasolabial folds, greasy scale)

first_line.name: Emollient (Cornerstone)
first_line.drugs: Cetaphil cream | CeraVe moisturizing cream | Eucerin | Aveeno — apply immediately after bathing ('soak and seal')
first_line.notes: Liberal emollient use is THE cornerstone of eczema management. Apply to entire body immediately after bathing (within 3 minutes) to seal in moisture. Use unscented, fragrance-free products. Apply liberally QID. More is better. Can reduce topical steroid requirement significantly.
first_line.preferred_when: All patients with eczema — baseline maintenance

first_line.name: Topical Corticosteroid
first_line.drugs: Hydrocortisone 1% (face/folds/children) | Betamethasone valerate 0.1% (trunk/limbs) | Betamethasone dipropionate 0.05% (palms/soles/thick skin)
first_line.notes: Match potency to site: LOW (hydrocortisone 1%) for face, eyelids, groin, axilla; MODERATE (betamethasone valerate 0.1%) for trunk and extremities. Apply fingertip unit amount to affected areas BID during flare. Once-weekly proactive therapy reduces relapse. Do not use high-potency on face/skin folds.

second_line.name: Topical Calcineurin Inhibitor
second_line.drugs: Tacrolimus 0.03%/0.1% ointment (Protopic) BID | Pimecrolimus 1% cream (Elidel) BID
second_line.notes: Non-steroidal for face, eyelids, skin folds (areas where TCS causes atrophy). Burning/stinging on application (transient, improves). Useful for maintenance in sensitive areas. FDA BBW (theoretical malignancy — reassure patients with decades of safety data showing no increased risk).

non_pharm: Identify and avoid triggers: soaps, detergents, fragrances, wool, extreme temperatures, stress, sweating
Lukewarm (not hot) bath/shower — brief; pat dry (do NOT rub)
Bleach bath 2× weekly (1/2 cup bleach in full tub) — reduces S. aureus colonization
Keep nails trimmed short (reduces scratch damage)
treatment.refer_when: Moderate-severe eczema not controlled with TCS; suspected ACD (patch testing needed); secondary infection; consideration for dupilumab (Dupixent); eczema herpeticum
